key_points.text: Compute Networking segment revenue nearly doubled year-over-year to $193.5 billion (FY2026) from $116.2 billion (FY2025), demonstrating exceptional growth driven by Data Center accelerated computing and AI solutions demand.

citation.quote: Compute Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing and networking platforms and AI solutions and software... Revenue $ 193,479 [FY2026] vs $ 116,193 [FY2025]

key_points.text: NVIDIA powers over 78% of supercomputers on the global TOP500 list and 9 of the top 10 systems on the Green500 list, indicating dominant market position in high-performance computing infrastructure.

citation.quote: NVIDIA powers over 78% of the supercomputers on the global TOP500 list, including 9 of the top 10 systems on the Green500 list

key_points.text: Data Center revenue grew substantially to $193.7 billion in FY2026 from $115.2 billion in FY2025, with Compute component revenue reaching $162.4 billion, reflecting strong AI and HPC workload acceleration demand.

In Q1 FY2027, gross margin improved significantly from 60.5% to 74.9%, with gross margin benefiting from lower inventory provisions due to non-recurrence of the prior year's $4.5 billion H20 excess inventory charge.

citation.quote: Gross margin increased from a year ago on lower inventory provisions, primarily due to the prior year's $4.5 billion charge associated with H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations

key_points.text: Blackwell architecture is driving strong demand momentum, with Blackwell systems accounting for the majority of system shipments and supporting robust demand across Data Center, Edge Computing, and Graphics segments.

citation.quote: Revenue growth in the first quarter was driven by data center products for accelerated computing and AI solutions. Blackwell continued to account for the majority of our system shipments

key_points.text: Edge Computing revenue reached $6.4 billion in Q1 FY2027, up 29% year-over-year and 10% sequentially, driven by robust Blackwell workstation demand despite slower consumer PC demand.

citation.quote: Edge Computing revenue for the first quarter was $6.4 billion, up 29% from a year ago and up 10% sequentially

key_points.text: Operating income in Compute Networking more than doubled to $53.3 billion in Q1 FY2027 from $22.1 billion in Q1 FY2026 (142% increase), reflecting strong revenue growth and operational leverage.

citation.quote: Compute Networking segment operating income in the first quarter of fiscal year 2027... $ 53,335 [Q1 FY2027] vs $ 22,054 [Q1 FY2026]

key_points.text: United States revenue concentration increased significantly, accounting for $149.6 billion (69%) of total FY2026 revenue, up from $77.5 billion (59%) in FY2025, driven by U.S.-based Compute Networking segment demand.

citation.quote: United States $ 149,617 [FY2026] vs $ 77,482 [FY2025]... The increase in revenue to the United States for fiscal years 2026 and 2025 was primarily due to higher U.S.-based Compute Networking segment demand
